close_reason: Crate doc covers chained-mode footgun; README is generated from crate doc
description:
secretx-azure-kv/src/lib.rs:17 (crate docstring) explains that 'managed-identity' is recommended in production to prevent silent fallback. README.md says 'recommended in production' but does not name the failure mode (silent dev-credential fallback). A user skimming README to choose a mode will not learn what bad thing happens in chained mode.

Fix: copy the 'prevents silent fallback to developer credentials on transient MI failures' phrase into the README bullet for managed-identity mode. One-line edit, no behavior change.
title: README does not call out chained-mode production footgun like the crate doc does
